summary.intent: Package the Gossamer Electron app for distribution via Homebrew, bundling the server inside the app so users have a single install experience.
summary.outcome: A working GitHub Actions release pipeline was set up that builds signed-less macOS DMGs, publishes them to GitHub Releases, and auto-updates a Homebrew tap (nosman/homebrew-gossamer). The app installs via `brew install --cask gossamer` with a caveat for the Gatekeeper workaround.

repo: The project has two separate package.json files: root (server/gossamer-serve) and app/ (Electron frontend) — they were originally independent programs.
server.ts uses import.meta.url to resolve PROJECT_ROOT, which constrains how server files must be laid out inside the bundled app.
prisma was in devDependencies but is required at runtime for pushSchema — needed to be moved to production dependencies.
The Electron app checks for ELECTRON_RENDERER_URL to determine whether to spawn the bundled server (production) or skip it (dev).
Native modules (better-sqlite3, node-pty) require rebuilding against Electron's ABI via @electron/rebuild before packaging.

code.finding: utilityProcess.fork() has reliability issues with dynamic ESM imports on macOS; replaced with child_process.spawn using ELECTRON_RUN_AS_NODE=1 on the Electron binary itself — the technique used by VS Code.
CJS wrapper file needed to bridge utilityProcess.fork() (CJS-only entry point) to the ESM server via dynamic import().
Must include a repository field for electron-builder to auto-detect the GitHub repo for publish config; without it the build fails with 'Cannot detect repository'.
GitHub Actions GITHUB_TOKEN defaults to read permissions — must explicitly declare `permissions: contents: write` on the job for release creation to succeed.

workflow: electron-builder must be invoked with --publish never when a separate upload step (softprops/action-gh-release) handles artifact uploading, otherwise it tries to publish itself and fails without a pre-existing release.
Heredocs inside YAML run: | blocks cause YAML parse errors — use sed substitution against a template file instead.
Homebrew Cask files must live in a Casks/ subdirectory of the tap repo.
Fine-grained GitHub PATs for pushing to a tap repo only need Contents: Read and write + Metadata: Read-only on the target repo.
app/package.json version must be synced to the git tag before building, otherwise DMG filenames (e.g. Gossamer-1.0.0-arm64.dmg) won't match the tag version used in Cask URLs.

Unsigned/unnotarized Electron apps on macOS show 'damaged and can't be opened' — xattr -cr /Applications/Gossamer.app is the user-facing workaround; adding it as a Homebrew caveats stanza surfaces it automatically post-install.

friction: package-lock.json was out of sync with package.json after adding electron-builder, causing npm ci to fail in CI.
Changes were on the README branch but network was down, requiring a worktree workaround to commit without checking out the branch.
Version mismatch between app/package.json (1.0.0) and git tag (v1.0.7) caused DMG filenames to not match Cask URLs, breaking brew install.
The cask auto-update step in the workflow never fired correctly on the first tagged release, leaving placeholder SHA256 values in the formula.
YAML heredoc in release.yml broke the pipeline and required rewriting the cask update step using sed against a template file.
The server failed to start on app launch due to utilityProcess.fork() ESM/CJS compatibility issues on macOS.
App showed 'damaged and can't be opened' on first launch due to missing code signing/notarization.
open_items: Code signing and notarization with an Apple Developer ID certificate ($99/yr) — currently users must run xattr -cr as a workaround.

Consolidating the repo structure (merging app/ and root src/ into a single package) was discussed and deferred — the separate layout creates friction with ../  paths in extraResources and two coordinated build steps.
The gossamer-serve CLI bin entry could still be preserved in package.json to support headless/server-only use cases if the repo is ever consolidated.
Reverting or parameterizing the workflow branch trigger (currently tags: ['v*']) should be verified — it was changed to branches during debugging and may need cleanup.
